The FOMC’s tone at the June meeting, together with Chairman Kevin Warsh’s news conference, has raised the prospect of a near-term rate hike.

items.title: Citadel Securities Warns of ‘Shifting Landscape’ Under Warsh Fed
items.summary: Investors are underestimating how determined Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh is to bring inflation back to the central bank’s 2% target and the likely drag that will create on risk assets, according to Citadel Securities.

items.title: With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: Iraq’s 14 Million Barrels of Trapped Oil Escape Through Hormuz
items.summary: Iraqi oil stranded by the Middle East conflict has escaped the Persian Gulf over the past 10 days as transit via the Strait of Hormuz eased during an uneasy US-Iran ceasefire.

items.title: Today, Denmark’s Minister of Defense Jeppe Bruus is in Ukraine for the first time since his appointment. This is a very important signal of support for our people. We discussed preparations for the Drone Deal. Our teams have been working on the agreement, and it is almost ready. The Minister and I share the view that the Drone Deal between Ukraine and Denmark should be concluded as soon as possible. We paid special attention to joint work on anti-ballistic capabilities. Europe’s capabilities in this area must be strengthened. We also discussed the contents of the next, already 30th, support package. I am grateful for the assurances that Denmark’s new government will continue its policy of strong support for Ukraine and our people.
items.summary:
